source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 5.3 yum-utils package (pp. 74-76)

questions.q: What is the yum-utils package on Rocky Linux 8+?
options: A collection of utilities that extend yum/dnf; still named yum-utils (also installable as dnf-utils), implemented as CLI shims over DNF
The core package manager that replaced dnf
questions.why: yum was replaced by dnf in Rocky 8, but the utility collection kept the yum-utils name (available as dnf-utils too) as backwards-compatible shims on top of DNF.

questions.q: What is the repoquery command used for?
options: Querying the packages in the repositories (dependencies, provided files, etc.)
Installing a package and its dependencies
questions.why: repoquery interrogates repository packages - whether or not they are installed - for information such as requires and file lists.

questions.q: Show the dependencies (requires) of the samba package with repoquery.
questions.why: repoquery --requires lists a package's dependencies; it is equivalent to 'dnf deplist samba'.

questions.q: List the files provided by the installed package yum-utils using repoquery.
questions.why: repoquery -l lists the files a package provides (installed packages only) - equivalent to 'rpm -ql yum-utils'.

options: Downloads RPM packages from the repositories without installing them
Installs packages from a download server
questions.why: yumdownloader fetches RPMs from the repos (equivalent to 'dnf download --downloadonly'), handy for building a small local repo.

questions.q: Download the samba RPM AND its dependencies into /var/tmp using yumdownloader.
questions.why: --destdir sets the download folder and --resolve additionally pulls the package's dependencies.
